behaviors.description: Keyword-frequency analyzer assigns correct sentiment when review contains only configured positive/negative words
behaviors.justification: This is pure deterministic logic localized to a single function. Testing with configured word lists and reviews will catch threshold errors (>= vs >), counting bugs, and precedence issues. No I/O, external dependencies, or non-determinism. Unit test infrastructure is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test that calls the sentiment analyzer with reviews containing known positive and negative words, verifying it produces the correct sentiment label. Test threshold boundary cases (e.g., exactly at threshold, one above, one below) and verify the function uses correct comparison operators.

behaviors.description: Explanation analyzer produces reasoning referencing specific phrases from the original review, not generic boilerplate
behaviors.justification: Cannot test without running the actual explanation analyzer—mocking would always pass even if real implementation returns generic templates. If the analyzer is rule-based, Integration suffices (run real analyzer, verify specific phrases appear). If LLM-based (likely given 'produces natural language reasoning'), the minimum is Agentic (LLM non-determinism and sophistication matter). Either way, real analyzer infrastructure is required.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test (or Agentic if LLM-based) that calls the actual explanation analyzer with a known review (e.g., 'The UI is confusing and slow'), then verifies the explanation output contains specific phrases from the review ('confusing', 'slow') and avoids generic boilerplate like 'the customer was unhappy'. Run multiple reviews to ensure phrase extraction is systematic.

behaviors.description: When review contains mixed signals (both positive and negative keywords), sentiment reflects the configured tie-breaking rule
behaviors.justification: Tie-breaking is part of the keyword analyzer's core logic—pure, deterministic, fully localized. Testing with reviews containing both positive and negative keywords will catch failures in rule application (wrong precedence, wrong operator, rule not consulted). No I/O or non-determinism. Unit test is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test that calls the sentiment analyzer with reviews containing both positive and negative keywords, testing: (1) tie case—equal counts of each (verify tie-breaker decides); (2) imbalanced case—more positive than negative (verify rule applied if configured); (3) threshold edge cases—reviews with counts near tie or at decision boundaries. Verify the configured tie-breaking precedence is actually applied.

behaviors.description: Analyzer correctly identifies negative sentiment in sarcastic review despite positive surface words (e.g., 'Great, another broken feature')
behaviors.justification: Cannot isolate in unit test—need the actual analyzer. Mocking hides whether sarcasm is genuinely detected or if logic is missing. Sarcasm detection requires language model sophistication (~60% likely LLM-based), so real minimum is probably Agentic. At minimum, Integration requires running actual analyzer with sarcastic input, catching failures where sarcasm is ignored.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test (or Agentic if LLM-based) that passes the actual analyzer a sarcastic review ('Great, another broken feature'), verifying it produces negative sentiment. Also test near-miss cases: non-sarcastic positive reviews ('Great service') should still be positive. Verify the analyzer detects linguistic cues (contradiction between positive adjective and negative context) or semantic patterns.

behaviors.description: Both outputs (score and explanation) returned together in single response, with explanation sentiment matching score sentiment direction
behaviors.justification: Tests the coupling and contract between sentiment and explanation components. Cannot mock either component—mocking hides integration failures (e.g., responses created independently without aggregation, or responses aggregated incorrectly). Requires both components running together, output being aggregated into a single structure, and sentiment alignment being verified. Real failure: score says negative but explanation discusses positive aspects.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that calls the complete feedback processing service (or API endpoint) with a review, verifying: (1) response contains both sentiment score and explanation in a single structure; (2) explanation sentiment direction (positive/negative cues) matches the score; (3) response structure matches spec (e.g., {score: 'negative', explanation: 'The customer expressed frustration...'} not separate responses). Test with mixed-signal reviews where coupling matters.
